text: It's borderline meta-comedic how 1:1 the narrative structure is to a typical battle shonen. We got the typical evil council mentioned, the big bad guy who "killed" the protagonist's father, a tournament arc, some unorthodox training )like the waitress part-time job), the supportive mentors of all kinds (aloof, stuck up, unserious but ranked the highest, the normal one), etc, etc, etc... It's as if classical battle shonen were a rakugo classic, that Akane-Banashi retells as performer.
label: analysis

text: His kneejerk would be to remove the system but soften to ensuring the system is as ethical as possible. He would know the two are incredibly capable of handling the risks their job entails, considering they have done the job without him noticing, meaning the tasks are trivial to them. Plus them being good people, means while reversed and the adults are supposed to take the risks, he can trust since the system is in good hands. Plus it would mean that everyone else in the café is involved, so the support is there. He would as an adult focus on what support they can give the Lycoris to mitigate risks, and try to improve the system, but I don't think he would want it torn down, after thinking about it. I keep repeating it, but it just works so well in how the show does it. Fair, the proper priority is adults should help, not dictate. I get that, especially if you see Chisato as a teenager. I trust her to make the decisions she has made, mainly because she guides Takina so flawlessly already so it is harder for me to see that she is in fact still a teenager.
label: analysis

text: Shiguma is such a charming man, I really enjoyed him going around town and chatting with townsfolk, you can really see how likable, respected grounded he is. ﻿ ﻿I also particularly liked the walk in the second half because it felt a lot more sentimental with him looking back on the past. He really feels like a second father, or a grandfather to Akane, it was certainly moving for me. I'm glad he understood Akane so well that he knew what name he should pick for her before she even made that plea. ﻿ ﻿I like how Jumbo and Akane's two friends are all getting motivated thanks to the tournament she participated in, it made me think how important it is to surround yourself with people that also help you grow. ﻿ ﻿I'm looking forward to Season 2, I'm really enjoying the story and I'm looking forward to how Akane will handle herself as an apprentice, but also interacting with other rakugoka. It's unfortunate that Isshou's haters will go after her when they don't even know that the two aren't on good terms lol.
label: analysis

text: Portraying Asa and Gabby as these cute, quirky girls despite committing a massacre was really the worst choice the mangaka made. Asa having a brother complex is also nauseating, like I can understand Asa having those feelings but the show goes over the top in portraying it, the only scene that I felt was tone appropriate was when Asa hugged Yuru as he left the Kagemori estate. If only the mangaka stopped portraying Asa's bro-con as a gag or comedy and more as this bittersweet longing, then maybe Asa would be more palatable. The tonal whiplash is my main problem with this show.
label: analysis

text: \>The Tabitha arc lasted a good six episodes, seven if you consider the end of episode two. I disagree. I would argue the Tabitha arc starts with her being kidnapped. That's the end of episode 8. \> The rune stuff at the beginning of the season should've been four episodes. It was a mistake doing it in two. \> They instead did some lowest common denominator comedy \[...\]. \> I would've had the rune stuff be four episodes including a \[...\] condensed version of episode 3, then I solace combined 4 and 5 \[..\], then I would've done something to episode 6 to where you kept the basic premise but maybe you do something more plot significant. No way. That sounds awful. I know I complained about the familiar pact disappearing not lasting long enough early on but dragging that out another two episodes and cutting out most of the first half is a terrible idea. Episode 3 lets us get to know Tifania better and episode 4 is straight up the best episode of the season. Episode 5 is whatever given the OVA exists but then you're talking about changing episode 6 which is one of the funniest episodes of the entire show so far. This season is already the weakest, the changes you're proposing would make it even worse.
label: analysis

text: I wonder how many people actually know about the Lycoris, if the president of the railways doesn't. Seems to be a pretty exclusive group. Also partly explains how DA can be run by imbeciles and not be called to task for it. And more butt kicking! Chisato being excited to play tour guide for a dying old man is so in character, though now I'm wondering what she meant by her heart, if it was a metaphor or if she's really got a mechanical one... I assume we'll find out during the season if not this episode. The tour guide bit is great because I'm learning things since I assume this is based on real places and facts. Feels like the sorta anime that would do that (for those who don't watch it for some crazy reason, Botan Kamiina is fantastic for showing off real places and a redditor keeps posting comparison pictures as they do a Kamiina pilgrimage of sorts). Oh hey cleanup missed a bullet. Oopsie. And Chisato really does see all, it is why her bullet dodging is as OP as it is. You can't catch her by surprise unless you're sniping from a massive distance away (if then). Last episode Takina demands to see Chisato's panties, this episode she goes for her breasts. Thirsty, thirsty Sakana! The music once Jin shows up... fuck I love the OST for this show. Bumping techno for tense scenes, combat or otherwise, always gets me grooving. ...Why did the target leave? Wut? Oh. And poor Mizuki she's not made for field work (or dating). Chisato fucking BELTED Jin between the punch and the bullet
